exercises.title: HTTP status codes for auth vs authz
exercises.scenario: 401 and 403 have distinct meanings in security.
exercises.instructions:
Match each scenario to the correct HTTP status code (401 or 403):

A. Request has no Authorization header
B. Request has an expired JWT token
C. Request has a valid JWT but user role is VIEWER, endpoint needs ADMIN
D. Request has a JWT signed with the wrong secret
E. Request has a valid JWT, user is authenticated but tries to access another user's data
exercises.expected_solution:
A → 401 (no credentials)
B → 401 (invalid/expired token = unauthenticated)
C → 403 (authenticated but wrong role)
D → 401 (invalid token = unauthenticated)
E → 403 (authenticated but unauthorized for that resource)

test_cases.expected:
A → 401 (no credentials)
B → 401 (invalid/expired token = unauthenticated)
C → 403 (authenticated but wrong role)
D → 401 (invalid token = unauthenticated)
E → 403 (authenticated but unauthorized for that resource)

hints: 401: who are you? (unauthenticated)
403: I know who you are, but you can't do this (unauthorized)

exercises.title: SecurityFilterChain configuration
exercises.scenario: The SecurityFilterChain defines which endpoints need authentication.
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blanks to complete the SecurityFilterChain.

blanks.hint: Turns off CSRF protection for REST APIs
No HTTP sessions — JWT is the only state
No authentication required for this path

hints: csrf.disable(): no sessions means no CSRF risk
STATELESS: JWT APIs don't use HTTP sessions

exercises.title: Decode a JWT payload (conceptual)
exercises.scenario: JWT payloads are base64-encoded JSON — not encrypted.
exercises.instructions:
Write a method `decodePayload(String jwtToken)` that:
1. Splits the token by '.'
2. Decodes the second part (payload) with Base64
3. Parses it as JSON with Jackson
4. Returns the 'sub' (subject) field as a String

This demonstrates that JWT payloads are readable — but NOT modifiable without the secret.
hints: Base64.getUrlDecoder() for JWT (URL-safe base64, not standard)
Split by '.': [header, payload, signature]
Anyone can decode the payload — only the signature is secret

exercises.title: JwtService — generate and extract username
exercises.scenario: JwtService wraps jjwt library for token operations.
exercises.instructions:
Write the core `JwtService` methods:
1. `generateToken(String username)` — creates a JWT with subject and 24h expiry
2. `extractUsername(String token)` — reads subject from token

Use a hardcoded secret for this exercise (in production: use @Value).
hints: Jwts.builder().subject().issuedAt().expiration().signWith().compact()
Jwts.parser().verifyWith(key).build().parseSignedClaims(token).getPayload()
Keys.hmacShaKeyFor(base64DecodedBytes) creates the signing key

exercises.title: Validate JWT — isValid method
exercises.scenario: Validation checks username match and expiry.
exercises.instructions:
Add `isValid(String token, UserDetails userDetails)` to JwtService. It should:
1. Extract the username from the token
2. Check username matches userDetails.getUsername()
3. Check the token is not expired
4. Return false (not throw) if any JwtException occurs
exercises.starter_code:
// Add to JwtService (build on Ex 4)
// isValid(String token, UserDetails userDetails) → boolean
hints: try-catch JwtException: return false for any invalid token
expiry.after(new Date()): true if not yet expired
Return false, not throw — the filter handles the unauthenticated state

exercises.title: JwtAuthFilter skeleton
exercises.scenario: The filter extracts the JWT and sets authentication.
exercises.instructions:
Write the `JwtAuthFilter` class skeleton with:
1. Extends OncePerRequestFilter
2. doFilterInternal that extracts Bearer token from Authorization header
3. Calls jwtService.extractUsername()
4. Sets authentication if valid

Leave the UserDetails loading as a comment placeholder.
hints: OncePerRequestFilter: guaranteed to run once per request
substring(7): removes 'Bearer ' prefix (7 characters including space)
chain.doFilter(req, res): must call at end to continue the filter chain

exercises.title: UserDetailsService implementation
exercises.scenario: Spring Security needs to load users from your database.
exercises.instructions:
Implement `AppUserDetailsService` that:
1. Implements UserDetailsService
2. Loads AppUser from AppUserRepository by username
3. Throws UsernameNotFoundException if not found
4. Returns Spring's User.builder() with username, hashed password, and role
hints: UsernameNotFoundException: specific exception Spring Security catches
User.builder().roles('ADMIN'): Spring adds ROLE_ADMIN prefix
Password must be BCrypt hashed — pass the hashed value

exercises.title: @WebMvcTest with @WithMockUser
exercises.instructions:
Write @WebMvcTest tests for a secured `ProductController`:
1. GET /api/products — no auth needed → 200
2. POST /api/products — needs auth, no @WithMockUser → 401
3. POST /api/products — with @WithMockUser → 201
4. DELETE /api/products/P001 — with @WithMockUser(roles='ADMIN') → 204
hints: @WithMockUser: simulates authenticated user with USER role
@WithMockUser(roles='ADMIN'): simulates ADMIN user
@MockBean JwtService and UserDetailsService: needed even in @WebMvcTest because JwtAuthFilter uses them

exercises.title: AuthController — login endpoint
exercises.scenario: The login endpoint validates credentials and returns a JWT.
exercises.instructions:
Write `AuthController.login(@RequestBody LoginRequest req)` that:
1. Uses AuthenticationManager to verify credentials
2. Loads UserDetails for the authenticated user
3. Calls jwtService.generateToken(username, roles)
4. Returns 200 with AuthResponse(token, username, roles)
5. On bad credentials: AuthenticationException → 401
hints: authManager.authenticate() throws BadCredentialsException on wrong password
Catch AuthenticationException (parent of BadCredentialsException)
getAuthority() returns 'ROLE_ADMIN' — roles includes the ROLE_ prefix

exercises.title: JWT claim structure — what's in the payload
exercises.scenario: JWT payload contains standard and custom claims.
exercises.instructions:
A JWT is generated with:
```java
Jwts.builder()
    .subject("aisha")
    .claim("role", "ADMIN")
    .claim("userId", "U-001")
    .issuedAt(new Date(1710000000000L))   // some timestamp
    .expiration(new Date(1710086400000L)) // 24h later
    .signWith(key)
    .compact();
```

What fields does the decoded payload JSON contain? List all fields and their values.
hints: subject() → 'sub' claim
issuedAt() → 'iat' (in seconds, not milliseconds)
expiration() → 'exp' (in seconds)
.claim('role','ADMIN') → custom claim 'role'

exercises.title: Get current user in controller
exercises.scenario: Authenticated user identity is available via Spring Security context.
exercises.instructions:
Write a `UserProfileController` with:
1. `GET /api/me` → returns currently logged-in user's profile (uses Authentication parameter)
2. `GET /api/users/{id}` → returns user by id, but only if it's the requesting user OR an ADMIN
3. Returns 403 if a non-admin user requests another user's profile
hints: auth.getName(): the username (JWT subject)
auth.getAuthorities(): Collection<GrantedAuthority> — check for ROLE_ADMIN
Return 403 (FORBIDDEN) when user tries to access another user's data

exercises.title: Role-based endpoint security
exercises.scenario: Different roles get different access levels.
exercises.instructions:
Configure a SecurityFilterChain for an e-commerce API:
- GET /api/products/** → public
- POST/PUT /api/products/** → requires SELLER or ADMIN role
- DELETE /api/products/** → requires ADMIN only
- GET /api/orders/** → requires authentication
- POST /api/orders → requires USER or ADMIN
- GET /api/admin/** → requires ADMIN only
- /api/auth/** → public

Write the complete authorizeHttpRequests block.
hints: hasAnyRole('A','B'): accepts either role
Order matters: more specific rules first (GET /api/products before anyRequest)
HttpMethod.GET etc: restrict by HTTP method

exercises.title: Register endpoint + BCrypt password hashing
exercises.scenario: User registration hashes the password before saving.
exercises.instructions:
Write `AuthController.register(@RequestBody RegisterRequest req)` that:
1. Validates username not taken (throw 409 if exists)
2. Hashes the password with BCryptPasswordEncoder
3. Saves the new AppUser with role USER
4. Returns 201 with the created user (without the password field)
5. Immediately logs them in and returns a JWT
hints: passwordEncoder.encode(rawPassword): BCrypt hash — different each time
Never return the password field in the response
409 Conflict for duplicate username

exercises.title: @SpringBootTest security integration test
exercises.scenario: Integration test the full login → use token flow.
exercises.instructions:
Write @SpringBootTest(RANDOM_PORT) tests that:
1. Register a user
2. Login with that user → receive a JWT
3. Access a protected endpoint with the JWT → 200
4. Access the same endpoint without a token → 401
5. Access an ADMIN endpoint with USER token → 403
hints: headers.setBearerAuth(token): sets 'Authorization: Bearer {token}' header
rest.exchange(): for non-GET methods or when you need to set headers
userToken from @BeforeEach login response — extract 'token' field

exercises.title: Complete JWT auth system — Module 14.1 synthesis
exercises.scenario: Full JWT authentication stack for the Zerodha-style trading API.
exercises.instructions:
Build a complete JWT-secured trading API:
- AppUser @Entity (id, username, password, role)
- AppUserDetailsService
- JwtService (generate with roles claim, validate, extract username + roles)
- JwtAuthFilter
- SecurityConfig: /api/auth/** public; GET /api/market/** public; POST /api/orders requires USER/ADMIN; DELETE /api/orders/** requires ADMIN
- AuthController with login and register
- @WebMvcTest tests: public endpoint (no auth), protected endpoint (no auth 401), protected (with @WithMockUser 200), admin-only (USER role 403)

All tests must pass.
hints: @MockBean JwtService and AppUserDetailsService: needed because JwtAuthFilter references them
@WithMockUser(roles='USER'): simulates USER role; @WithMockUser(roles='ADMIN') for admin
status().isUnauthorized()=401; status().isForbidden()=403
